channel.title: NYT > Science
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: A Physicist Who Thinks in Poetry from the Cosmic Edge
item.description: In her second pop-science book, theoretical cosmologist Chanda Prescod-Weinstein returns to her celestial and cultural roots.
item.dc:creator: Katrina Miller
item.category: The Disordered Cosmos: A Journey into Dark Matter, Spacetime, and Dreams Deferred (Book)
item.media:credit: Cassandra Klos for The New York Times

item.title: Building Nests Is Hard. That’s Why Some Birds Steal.
item.description: When researchers used GPS devices to monitor the nests of Hawaiian honeycreepers, they found dozens of cases of brazen avian burglary.
item.dc:creator: Sara Novak

item.title: How an ‘Impossible’ Idea Led to a Pancreatic Cancer Breakthrough
item.description: The new strategy also holds promise for lung and colon tumors. Here’s how scientists discovered it.
item.dc:creator: Gina Kolata and Rebecca Robbins
item.media:credit: Sophie Park for The New York Times

item.title: A Taxidermist Gives Dead Animals a New Life
item.description: The creation, care and keeping of creatures is a responsibility the last full-time museum taxidermist in the U.S. takes both seriously and joyfully.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Kisner and Sasha Arutyunova
item.category: Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County

item.title: A Fish That Hitches Rides Where the Sun Doesn’t Shine
item.description: The remora often latches on to the exteriors of larger marine creatures. But sometimes it travels in a more intrusive spot: inside a manta ray’s backside.
item.dc:creator: Jason Bittel
item.media:credit: Bryant Turffs/Marine Megafauna Foundation
item.media:description: Traditionally, experts have seen the relationship between remoras and manta rays as either commensal or mutualistic, but this arrangement may veer into the parasitic.

item.title: Scientists Press Congress on Dismissal of National Science Foundation Board and Research Funding
item.description: The slowed pace of grants by the National Science Foundation, under attack from the White House, could put the United States at a disadvantage with China, the scientists warned.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella
item.media:credit: Mark Schiefelbein/Associated Press
item.media:description: The headquarters of the National Science Foundation in May last year, before the Trump administration announced it was moving the agency out of the building.

item.title: U.S.-China Rivalry Reaches South American Skies
item.description: The U.S. has pressed Argentina and Chile to review two Chinese telescope projects in the Andean deserts. Astronomers are worried about setbacks to research.
item.dc:creator: Emma Bubola, Edward Wong and Sarah Pabst
item.media:description: The unfinished Chinese telescope at night at the Cesco observatory in El Leoncito, in Argentina’s San Juan Province.

item.title: She Tried to Help Schools Build Healthier Playgrounds. Then Her E.P.A. Grant Was Canceled.
item.description: Kirsten Beyer was assessing the benefits of improving school playgrounds in Milwaukee. Then her E.P.A. grant was canceled.
item.dc:creator: Emily Anthes
item.media:credit: Sara Stathas for The New York Times

item.title: David Attenborough, a Voice of Nature, Turns 100
item.description: Pictures and striking scenes from the making of perhaps the world’s most celebrated naturalist.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Wolfe
item.media:credit: John Stillwell/Press Association, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Mr. Attenborough enrolled at the University of Cambridge in 1945, where he studied natural sciences, including geology and zoology.

item.title: Black Bear Fatally Mauls Uranium Contractor in Northern Canada
item.description: The attack, at a remote uranium mining site in northern Saskatchewan, was only the fourth fatal black bear encounter in the province’s recorded history, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Adeel Hassan
item.media:credit: Victor R. Caivano/Associated Press
item.media:description: The black bear is the most common species in Canada, though bear attacks are rare.

item.title: Nancy Cox, Who Worked to Conquer the Wily Flu, Dies at 77
item.description: As the leader of the C.D.C.’s influenza division, she battled to keep up with an ever-changing viral opponent, building a global network of researchers and forecasters.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

item.title: Rich Danker, RFK Jr.’s Top Spokesman, Resigns in Protest Over New Vaping Policy
item.description: In a letter to President Trump, the spokesman, Rich Danker, said allowing the sale of flavored e-cigarettes would enhance their appeal to children.
item.dc:creator: Sheryl Gay Stolberg
item.media:credit: Michael A. McCoy for The New York Times
item.media:description: The resignation comes at a fraught time for the Department of Health and Human Services, which now has three major vacancies.

item.title: With a Friend in Trump, the Tobacco Industry Secures a Lucrative Win
item.description: In a dispute over vapes, the president sided with tobacco companies that filled his groups’ coffers over his own F.D.A. commissioner, who resigned in protest.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Kenneth P. Vogel
item.media:credit: Getty Images
item.media:description: The F.D.A. issued new guidance that could pave the way for major tobacco companies to begin selling flavored vapes less than a week after industry executives met with President Trump.

item.title: 5 Great Stargazing Trains
item.description: On nighttime excursions in dark-sky hot spots like Norway, New Zealand and Nevada, all you have to do is relax and look up. The stars will do the rest.
item.dc:creator: Michael Harmon

item.title: Lithuania’s Peat Bogs Could Help the Climate and Defend the Border, Too
item.description: Lithuanian officials hope restored peat bogs can reinforce the border in addition to locking away planet-warming carbon.
item.dc:creator: Avril Silva

item.title: Marty Makary, Trump’s F.D.A. Commissioner, Resigns After Weeks of Pressure
item.description: The agency’s top food official will step in to the role after Dr. Makary privately said he opposed the administration decision to approve flavored vapes.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett
item.media:credit: Al Drago for The New York Times
item.media:description: Dr. Marty Makary at times tried to advance his own principles alongside the administration’s goals, which at times were at odds with the powerful food, tobacco and pharmaceutical industries.

item.title: Trump Nominates Cameron Hamilton to Lead FEMA
item.description: President Trump chose Cameron Hamilton to direct federal disaster response. As acting head of FEMA last year, he had opposed abolishing the agency and was ousted.
item.dc:creator: Scott Dance
item.media:credit: Amanda Andrade-Rhoades for The Washington Post, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Cameron Hamilton campaigning in 2024 during his unsuccessful run as a Republican for a congressional seat in Virginia.

item.title: RFK Jr. Is Driving a Vast Inquiry Into Vaccines, Despite His Public Silence
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has toned down his public criticism of vaccines, under orders from the White House. But inside his department, a sprawling research effort is a top priority.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Sheryl Gay Stolberg
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times

item.title: A Single Infusion Could Suppress H.I.V. for Years, Study Suggests
item.description: A study of a few patients, to be presented this week, showed promise for a type of therapy that has already cured some blood cancers.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.media:credit: Justin Sullivan/Getty Images
item.media:description: A study led by an H.I.V. expert at the University of California, San Francisco, could offer a path toward a lasting H.I.V. treatment.

item.title: With Commissioner Under Pressure, F.D.A. Opens Door to Flavored Vapes
item.description: Though illicit e-cigarettes have flooded in from China, the new policy could allow major tobacco companies to sell from prime shelf space at thousands of stores.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett
item.media:credit: Justin J Wee for The New York Times
item.media:description: Rates of adolescent vaping have fallen steadily in recent years.

item.title: Hantavirus Vaccines and Treatments Are in the Pipeline
item.description: But it has been hard to attract interest in medical interventions for viruses that have not been considered a top public health priority, scientists say.
item.dc:creator: Emily Anthes
item.media:credit: Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: An ambulance boat approaching the cruise ship MV Hondius, which has sustained a deadly hantavirus outbreak, off Cape Verde on Tuesday.

item.title: U.F.O. Files Released by U.S. Shed Light on What the Government Knows
item.description: The Pentagon on Friday released online what it called “new, never-before-seen” files, dating back decades, related to unidentified flying objects.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levenson
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: The Pentagon, via Getty Images
item.media:description: An image of a U.F.O. resembling a football-shaped body near Japan that the U.S. Indo-Pacific Command reported.

item.title: Trump Plans to Fire F.D.A. Commissioner Marty Makary
item.description: Dr. Makary has been a supporter of the Make American Healthy Again Movement but made some enemies in the administration over vaping, the abortion pill and rejections of new drugs.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett and Maggie Haberman
item.media:credit: Eric Lee/The New York Times
item.media:description: Dr. Marty Makary, commissioner of the Food and Drug Administration, at the White House last year.

item.title: There Are Ants in This Canadian Hospital. Again.
item.description: A hospital in Manitoba had to postpone a “limited number of elective surgeries” after ants appeared there for the third time since 2024.
item.dc:creator: Sopan Deb
item.media:credit: Damon Winter/The New York Times
item.media:description: The provincial authority that runs Carman Memorial Hospital in Carman, Manitoba, said it was working with patients to reschedule elective surgeries after ants appeared in the operating room.

item.title: Greenpeace Suffers Another Blow in Court Fight With Energy Transfer
item.description: In an unusual move, a North Dakota court said Greenpeace International shouldn’t be allowed to pursue a lawsuit in Europe, where it is based, against the company.
item.dc:creator: Karen Zraick
item.media:credit: Thibaud Moritz/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Activists in France protested against the Energy Transfer lawsuit against Greenpeace USA last year.

item.title: Hantavirus Is Nothing Like Coronavirus, but It’s Bringing Some ‘Covid P.T.S.D.’
item.description: Experts have been quick to reassure the public after the deaths aboard the Dutch cruise ship MV Hondius, but images and turns of phrase have rekindled anxieties from Covid’s early days.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor
item.media:credit: Misper Apawu/Associated Press
item.media:description: Health workers in protective gear evacuating patients from the MV Hondius cruise ship yesterday.

item.title: What Is Body Dysmorphic Disorder?
item.description: It may look like vanity, but it’s a debilitating mental health condition.
item.dc:creator: Christina Caron

item.title: Pentagon Releases Files on U.F.O.s
item.description: The initial files are murky images that show what could be anything. The government said more would be released on a rolling basis.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

item.title: Trump Administration Lifts Ban on ‘Cyanide Bombs’ on Public Lands
item.description: The Bureau of Land Management will allow the use of the spring-loaded traps, which can kill coyotes and other animals that prey on livestock.
item.dc:creator: Maxine Joselow
item.media:credit: Bannock County Sheriff's Office, via Associated Press
item.media:description: An M-44 device, or “cyanide bomb,” used for predator control in Pocatello, Idaho.

item.title: Trump Panel Recommends FEMA Respond to Fewer Disasters
item.description: A White House task force called for speeding up aid and responding to fewer disasters. But some of its ideas would require action by Congress to become reality.
item.dc:creator: Scott Dance
item.media:credit: Loren Elliott for The New York Times
item.media:description: A FEMA staging area in Kerrville, Texas, last July, in the wake of the catastrophic flooding that destroyed an all-girls camp.

item.title: Hantavirus Response Shows How Trump Cuts Have Compromised U.S. Preparedness
item.description: The Trump administration has slashed funding for infectious disease research and has far fewer employees, including disease detectives, to respond to outbreaks.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Peter Dejong/Associated Press
item.media:description: An ambulance crew awaiting an evacuated passenger with a suspected hantavirus infection in Amsterdam on Wednesday. The U.S. government has released very little information about the outbreak on a cruise ship, which has affected several Americans.

item.title: Surprising Signs of an Atmosphere Around a Tiny World, Billions of Miles Away
item.description: A gradual dimming and brightening when a star passed behind it suggested the mini-Pluto was wrapped in a thin layer of air.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth Chang

item.title: Employees With Medical Conditions Challenge C.D.C. In-Office Requirement
item.description: The health agency has been particularly strict in abolishing at-home work, overriding accommodations that were granted years before the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: Katie Schenk, an infectious disease epidemiologist, was hired as a remote C.D.C. employee in 2024 and lost her telework accommodation. After trying in vain to get it back she quit the agency in frustration in February.

item.title: America the Undammed
item.description: More miles of the country’s rivers were reconnected last year thanks to dam removals than at any other time in history.
item.dc:creator: Cara Buckley
item.media:credit: Andrew Mangum for The New York Times

item.title: What My Father’s Experience Taught Me About Memory and the Brain
item.description: In the final stages of his dementia, a long-lost memory from childhood returned, perfectly formed. What was going on in his brain?
item.dc:creator: Ellen Barry and Graham Dickie
item.media:credit: Graham Dickie for The New York Times
